summary: Broad front-page HN product scout surfaced 10 concrete items after history dedupe.

items.name: FUTO Swipe
items.source_story_title: FUTO Swipe – A new swipe typing model
items.summary: A swipe-typing model for Android keyboards with strong autocorrect.
items.why_included: Commenters said its swipe and autocorrect feel competitive with Gboard.
items.evidence: The thread praised typing quality and the swipe demo, even while debating the FUTO license.

items.name: TikZ Editor
items.source_story_title: Show HN: TikZ Editor – WYSIWYG editor for figures in LaTeX
items.summary: A WYSIWYG editor that syncs TikZ source with rendered diagrams.
items.why_included: It drew concrete praise for live source/render sync and practical TikZ editing.
items.evidence: Commenters discussed the source-location mapping, relative positioning, and the difficulty of preserving user intent.

items.name: bun-sqlgen
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Bun-sqlgen – Type-safe raw SQL for Bun, no ORM
items.summary: A Bun SQL codegen tool that keeps raw SQL and adds types.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the raw-SQL-plus-types approach as a pragmatic alternative to ORMs.
items.evidence: The thread compared it favorably with sqlc, Kysely, and pgtyped while focusing on nullability and generated typings.

items.name: Plotnine
items.summary: A Python plotting library built around the ggplot2 grammar of graphics.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced strong interest in the grammar-of-graphics model and the new guide.
items.evidence: Commenters praised composability and publication-style plotting while comparing it with R and ggplot2.

items.name: Swift Package Index
items.summary: A Swift package registry/index that Apple is bringing into its ecosystem.
items.why_included: The Apple deal drew clear strategy discussion around registry support and maintainer payoff.
items.evidence: Comments framed the acquisition as a likely registry win and debated Apple’s developer-tooling track record.

items.name: Claude Tag
items.summary: A Slack-based collaborative Claude workspace with channel-scoped memory.
items.why_included: It was the clearest new Anthropic product in the front page and sparked real workflow debate.
items.evidence: Commenters were interested in the multiplayer Slack model but argued over RBAC, compliance, and memory boundaries.

items.name: Mistral OCR 4
items.summary: An OCR model for long documents and layout-heavy parsing.
items.why_included: Commenters used it to compare the current OCR stack and where model-based OCR still wins.
items.evidence: The thread compared it with PaddleOCR, Tesseract, Mathpix, Azure Document Intelligence, and other OCR tools.

items.name: Unlimited OCR
items.summary: A long-document OCR approach using reference-sliding-window attention.
items.why_included: It sparked detailed discussion of long-document parsing, memory growth, and local AI deployments.
items.evidence: Commenters liked the architecture for separating global reference from local generation and compared it to chunking and compaction.

items.name: Nemesis8
items.source_story_title: Elevated error rate across multiple models
items.summary: A containerized-agents runtime discussed as a build option alongside Pi.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended it as a containerized-agents build.
items.evidence: The outage thread mentioned Nemesis8 while discussing Pi and GLM 5.2 as alternatives for building agents.

items.name: Lift4D
items.source_story_title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
items.summary: A single-view 3D/4D reconstruction project for scene tracking.
items.why_included: The thread was enthusiastic about the reconstruction idea and asked for clearer comparisons and tooling.
items.evidence: Commenters asked about accuracy, compared it with sam-body4d, and wanted the code release sooner.
